channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hegseth renames North Carolina military base Fort Roland L. Bragg
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth signed an order Monday restoring the name of a storied special operations forces base back to Fort Bragg.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Recent aviation disasters cause fears about the safety of flying
item.description: The spate of recent aviation disasters and close calls have people worried about the safety of flying. The midair collision that killed 67 near Washington, the fiery plane crash in Philadelphia and a plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 are only the most high-profile disasters.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hegseth says Fort Bragg is coming back, but with a twist
item.description: On Monday, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth signed a memo that orders the Army to rename Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, this time in honor of a different soldier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trader Joe's, Smith's limit daily egg purchases due to nationwide shortages
item.description: Trader Joe's is limiting egg sales to one dozen per customer daily -- and it is not the only company doing so.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: US judge keeps Trump plan to push out federal workers on hold
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's plan to downsize the federal workforce remains on hold after a courtroom hearing on Monday afternoon. It's the latest example of how the Republican president's ambitious plans have become ensnared in the judicial system. U.S. District Judge George O’Toole Jr.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: No, you won't get your student loans forgiven if DOGE violated FERPA
item.description: No, you won't get your student loans forgiven if DOGE violated FERPA, even though TikTok videos are claiming otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Combat your post-Super Bowl fatigue with these freebies, discounts
item.description: Even though the Super Bowl is over, several restaurants and chains are offering freebies to help football fans recover from Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Researchers announce major discovery about Earth's inner core
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) – Researchers at the University of Southern California say they’ve unraveled the mystery of why Earth's inner core has been slowing, gradually altering the length of a day.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump says he has directed US Treasury to stop minting new pennies, citing rising cost
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says he has directed the Treasury Department to stop minting new pennies, citing the rising cost of producing the one-cent coin. “For far too long the United States has minted pennies which literally cost us more than 2 cents.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meta layoffs to begin as company moves to cut 5 percent of workforce
item.description: The layoffs, according to Zuckerberg, would target "low-performers." The layoffs will amount to roughly 3,600 people losing their jobs, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, NewsNation

item.title: What we know about Alaska plane crash victims, as families and colleagues remember them
item.description: Family members of the 10 killed in a commuter plane crash in Alaska are sharing memories online, while investigators keep looking into the cause of the Feb. 6, 2025, crash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deputies reunite with family they saved from crash with stolen vehicle
item.description: “It was a mother and two children. At which point I drove in front of the vehicle causing the vehicle to crash into me," said Connor Lacey.
item.dc:creator: Alliyah Sims, NewsNation

item.title: So long, penny! Trump orders US to ditch 1-cent coin after decades of complaints
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — The lowly penny, the forgotten mainstay of coin jars and car cupholders everywhere, may soon be no more. President Donald Trump announced Sunday he’s ordered his administration to cease production of the 1-cent coin, whose buying power is long past its prime.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family of Chesapeake man missing since last July still hoping for answers
item.description: "He's been gone six months now, and it's just hard to go on without him," Cynthia Wolf, Bill Wolf's wife said.
item.dc:creator: Cheyenne Pagan, NewsNation

item.title: Protester speaks after being hit by car during Salt Lake City demonstration
item.description: Padilla-Oliver said the driver slowed down and stopped, after which she "stood with pride" and held her sign.
item.dc:creator: MJ Jewkes, NewsNation

item.title: Judge finds Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending
item.description: A federal judge found Monday that the Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending and told the White House to release all the money.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What the Westminster show dogs' names mean
item.description: The dogs at the Westminster Kennel Club show have names that could make a random password generator cry uncle.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Buckeyes football coach Jim Tressel nominated as Ohio's lieutenant governor
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine on Monday nominated former Ohio State Buckeyes football coach Jim Tressel to be the state's next lieutenant governor, praising him as a seasoned educator and leader with the skills to take the state's helm if need be.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Taylor Swift booed by heavily pro-Eagles crowd at the Super Bowl
item.description: Taylor Swift was back at the Super Bowl to watch Travis Kelce and his Kansas City Chiefs play the Philadelphia Eagles on Sunday. Her reception was a whole lot different than last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump says he is serious about Canada becoming 51st state in Super Bowl interview
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said he is serious about wanting Canada to become the 51st state in an interview that aired Sunday during the Super Bowl preshow.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Court grants request to block detained Venezuelan immigrants from being sent to Guantanamo
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A federal court on Sunday blocked the Trump administration from sending three Venezuelan immigrants held in New Mexico to Guantanamo Bay Naval Base in Cuba as part of the president's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are focusing on the wreckage of a plane that crashed off western Alaska as they try to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea, killing 10 people.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Is the Super Bowl halftime singer lip syncing? Check for these signs
item.description: Casual fans will be watching the theatrics, choreography and guest appearances during Kendrick Lamar and SZA's Super Bowl halftime show. Sleuths will be watching their throats.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: PHOTOS: Celebrities spotted at the Super Bowl
item.description: The players on the field Sunday at Super Bowl LIX are celebrities in their own right, but they're far from the only big names gathered in New Orleans for the Chiefs-Eagles matchup.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Eagles beat Chiefs 40-22 in lopsided Super Bowl LIX
item.description: The Kansas City Chiefs wanted to make history with three back-to-back-to-back championships, but Super Bowl LIX was dominated by the Philadelphia Eagles, who won the game 40-22.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: US Marine from California identified as one of 4 people killed in Philippines plane crash
item.description: CAMP PENDLETON, Calif. (AP) — A U.S. Marine from California was identified by military officials Sunday as one of four people killed when a plane contracted by the U.S. military crashed in a rice field in the southern Philippines. Sgt. Jacob M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration orders consumer protection agency to stop work, closes building
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has ordered the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to stop nearly all its work, effectively shutting down an agency that was created to protect consumers after the 2008 financial crisis and subprime mortgage-lending scandal.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Efforts to put carbon dioxide underground face less support in Trump's second term
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — Putting carbon dioxide from power plants and industrial facilities underground where it won’t contribute to global warming could see less federal support and enthusiasm under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk calls for impeachment of judge who blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: Elon Musk is calling for the impeachment of the federal judge who made a decision early Saturday morning that the Treasury Department should block access to anyone “other than civil servants with a need for access to perform their job duties” from its payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Snow blankets parts of Northeast US, up to a foot expected for some
item.description: A strong storm is expected to bring moderate to heavy snowfall across parts of the Upper Great Lakes on Saturday before intensifying overnight across the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What makes a dog a winner? A Westminster Kennel Club judge explains
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The competitors can come in forms as different as a Chihuahua, a great Dane, a bulldog and a greyhound. Each one is a champion.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Canada beats US 3-1 in Game 5 to win the Rivalry Series women's hockey showcase
item.description: Jennifer Gardiner broke a tie with 6:44 left and Canada beat the United States 3-1 on Saturday night to win the Rivalry Series women's hockey showcase for the third straight time.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KRON4

item.title: Missing Alaska flight found with no survivors: What to know
item.description: The plane, operated by Bering Air, had been traveling from Unalakleet to Nome when it vanished on Thursday, only to be found the next day.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Philadelphia radio station bans Taylor Swift music ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: A release from the station claims that 94.5 is "pressing pause on all things Taylor Swift." The announcement came after a Swift fan and a Philly advertising agency spent the week trolling each other with ads in KC and Philly.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Members of Congress denied access to Department of Education
item.description: Multiple members of Congress were denied entry to the Department of Education on Friday, according to videos and social media posts from lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
